note: Reference-level (top_level=4, depth=3) worst-case covering multiplicity mult_g(r=3^g), g=1,2,3. Every walk is independently triple-free-verified (first_disqualifier==None asserted on the full chain at each level) => a legal orbit. g=1 (r=3, finest) is the most level-robust scale; g=3 at low top_level is finite-level-inflated (see L3 where even the design 'base' route showed g3=9 despite its true 4/5/4 at L7).
verdict: Legal (triple-free-verified) orbits EXCEED the measured cap of 5. At the reliable finest scale g=1: design route <=4, matched-density max-crowding adversary up to 6, wider-closure fold adversary up to 9. All remain << Prong B packing bound 612, so FINITENESS is intact; only the TIGHT constant (4/5/4) is broken. Covering multiplicity tracks the routing density lambda/d: at the design's own density (d~1.10) the worst case is ~5-6, and it climbs to ~9-11 only as longer legal stitches raise d to 1.28-1.46 (a denser self-similar system in the same menu closure). This confirms gap (i): the tight cap 5 is a property of the specific minimal-lambda short-route construction, not of the legal closure.
